svg.text: PalSwap: VGA DAC Entry Map for CGA Mode 4
All 16 DAC entries pre-loaded — every CGA palette variant gets the right user color
Dark Cyan / Dark Magenta / Dark White
CGA games select one of four palette variants via INT 10h AH=0Bh.
Each variant routes pixels to a different set of DAC entries.
PalSwap fills ALL 16 DAC entries before the game starts.
No matter which palette the game picks, our colors are already there.
When the game calls INT 10h AH=00 to set CGA mode 4, the BIOS normally reloads the default palette.
With 1201h/31h active, the BIOS skips this — our DAC values survive the mode switch.
The game then calls AH=0Bh to select its palette, which routes ATC to our pre-loaded DAC entries.
